page: Amazon Web Services m1.small is a first-generation general-purpose server instance. It features a single dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2651 v2 physical core operating at 1.8 GHz on the i386 architecture. The instance is provisioned with 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding 1.7 GB of memory per core, and runs on the Xen hypervisor. Local storage is bundled with the instance, consisting of a 160 GB hard disk drive (HDD). It does not include any GPU hardware or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and its baseline network bandwidth is rated at 0 Gbps.

Benchmark data places the performance of the m1.small in the bottom 10% across all tested categories. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU benchmarks both register at 969.19. Geekbench testing yields a single-core score of 490.0 and a multi-core score of 492.0, reflecting the single-threaded nature of the single-core allocation. Memory bandwidth is limited, with cached small-block reads reaching 66.65 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads dropping to 8.16 GB/sec. Cryptographic performance is aided by hardware AES support, though the CPU lacks modern instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512, and AMX.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m1.small represents a legacy, low-resource hardware profile. While it offers dedicated CPU allocation and bundled local HDD storage, its utility is constrained by legacy i386 architecture and low processing throughput. This server is best suited for legacy applications, basic utility scripting, low-traffic static web serving, or lightweight testing environments that do not require modern instruction sets or high network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services m1.small is a legacy first-generation general-purpose instance featuring a single dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2651 v2 core running at 1.8 GHz on the i386 architecture. Managed via the Xen hypervisor, it is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory and 160 GB of local HDD storage. The instance lacks GPU accelerators and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Performance benchmarks consistently rank in the bottom 10%, with a Geekbench single-core score of 490.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 949.92. Lacking modern instruction sets like AVX and AMX, its hardware AES support provides basic cryptographic capabilities. The m1.small is qualitatively positioned as a low-resource legacy option, suitable primarily for basic utility scripting, lightweight testing, or low-traffic static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m1.small is a legacy general-purpose instance featuring 1 vCPU, 2.0 GB RAM, and 160 GB HDD storage on the i386 architecture, suitable for basic utility workloads.

tagline: A legacy, low-resource general-purpose instance featuring a single dedicated core, bundled HDD storage, and basic utility processing capabilities.
